content: Find out the products of reaction
explanation:
The given reaction (as per the options) is crossed Cannizzaro reaction. A simple Cannizzaro reaction is shown by those aldehydes in which $\alpha$-hydrogen is absent. These aldehydes in the presence of a strong base like $$\mathrm{NaOH}$$ show disproportionation, i.e. simultaneous oxidation and reduction of the same molecule. Thus, the result includes an acid alongwith an alcohol. In crossed Cannizzaro reaction however, molecules of two different can participate, out of which the one more prone to nucleophilic attack will show oxidation, while the other show reduction. Here, out of the given two substrates, $$\mathrm{HCHO}$$ is more prone to nucleophilic attack hence, this will undergo oxidation while $$\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CHO}$$ will undergo reduction. Hence, the possible products are $$\mathrm{HCOO}^{-} \mathrm{Na}^{+}+\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{CH}_2 \mathrm{OH}$$.

Note Same combination of substrates give crossed aldol as well, in the presence of $$\mathrm{NaOH}$$ to form

But as, it is not among the options as the product, hence we are considering option (c) as the most appropriate option.

content:
Assertion : Anhydrides are more reactive than ester for nucleophilic substitution.

Reason : $$RCOO -$$ is better leaving group than $$\mathrm{R}-\mathrm{O}^{-}$$.

options.content: If both assertion and reason are true and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.
If both assertion and reason are true, but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion.
If assertion is true, but reason is false.
If both assertion and reason are false.

explanation:
Assertion:

Anhydrides are more reactive than esters for nucleophilic substitution.

Reason:

$$ RCOO^- $$ is a better leaving group than $$ \mathrm{R}-\mathrm{O}^{-} $$.

Explanation:

In nucleophilic acyl substitution reactions, the reactivity of the compounds depends on the leaving group's quality. A good leaving group is one that can stabilize the negative charge well when it departs from the molecule.

Anhydrides contain the $$ RCOO^- $$ group, while esters contain the $$ \mathrm{R}-\mathrm{O}^{-} $$ group. The $$ RCOO^- $$ group is more stable due to resonance stabilization, which disperses the negative charge over two oxygen atoms. This makes $$ RCOO^- $$ a better leaving group compared to $$ \mathrm{R}-\mathrm{O}^{-} $$, which does not have such extensive resonance stabilization. Therefore, anhydrides are more reactive in nucleophilic substitution reactions than esters.

Conclusion:

Both the assertion and the reason are true, and the reason provides the correct explanation for the assertion.

Correct Option: A

content: The relative reactivities of acyl compounds towards nucleophilic substitution are in the order of

options.content: acyl chloride > acid anhydride > ester > amide
ester > acyl chloride > amide > acid anhydride
acid anhydride > amide > ester > acyl chloride
acid chloride > ester > acid anhydride > amide

explanation:
The ease of nucleophilic substitution depends upon the nature of leaving group. When the leaving tendency of a group in a compound is high, then the compound is more reactive towards nucleophilic substitution. The nucleophilic acyl substitution is completed in two steps as shown below.
